name: Chicken Teriyaki
notes: Scale to 2 packs (8 thighs) for 8 servings. Marinate 30 min to overnight.
recipe_steps: Marinate thighs in dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, minced garlic, sweetener, five spice powder, and white pepper. Let sit 30 min to overnight.
Start 1 cup of rice in the rice cooker.
Air fry chicken at 360°F for 17 minutes.
Steam bok choy or gai lan for 11 minutes. Salt when done.
Divide into 4 containers: rice, 1 thigh, and veg.

name: Cheesy Chipotle Chicken Rice Bowls
notes: 585 cal / 52g protein / 57g carbs / 17g fat per serving. Air fryer only. From @stealth_health_life.
recipe_steps: Mix chicken thighs with avocado oil, hot honey, and all dry seasonings (chipotle/ancho chili powder, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, salt). Toss to coat evenly. Marinate in fridge — add lime juice just before cooking.
Air fry chicken at 375°F for 20–25 minutes, flipping halfway, until cooked through and lightly charred. Remove and chop into bite-sized pieces.

Remove air fryer rack. Add diced bell peppers, ½ onion, 2 tsp oil, and 1 Tbsp tomato paste directly to the basket. Air fry at 350°F for 10–15 minutes, stirring once or twice, until softened and lightly browned.

Add cooked rice to the veggies in the basket (or transfer to a large bowl). Stir in ½ cup mozzarella, ½ cup cheddar, and ½ cup Greek yogurt until melted and creamy. Add chopped chicken and cilantro. Mix until combined.
Whisk together sauce ingredients: ¾ cup Greek yogurt, ¼ cup milk, 1 tsp honey, 3 Tbsp adobo sauce, smoked paprika, and salt until smooth.
Divide into 5 equal servings and top each with smoky chipotle sauce.

name: Hoisin Chicken Thigh Chow Mein
notes: ON BREAK — rotate back in a future cycle. Adapted from Joshua Weissman. Protein doubled to 4 thighs, sauce scaled 1.5x, garlic bumped to 10 cloves. Veg quantities increased across the board. Dutch oven recommended given large volume. Optional toppings: fried egg, chili oil.
recipe_steps: Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, hoisin sauce, and sesame oil. Whisk in cornstarch until dissolved. Set sauce aside.
Season the wok: add a little vegetable oil, heat over medium-high until smoking, turn off heat, pour out oil, wipe down with paper towels.
Heat 3 Tbsp vegetable oil in wok over medium-high. Working in two batches, sear chicken until cooked through, stirring often, about 5–8 minutes per batch. Set aside.

Sauté green onions until charred and slightly wilted (~30 seconds). Add ¾ of the garlic, stir fry until golden. Add celery and carrot, stir fry until softened (~2 minutes). Raise to high heat, add cabbage and stir fry until charred and wilted (~2 minutes).
Return chicken to wok, reduce to medium-high, stir fry 30 seconds.
Add bean sprouts, stir fry 30 seconds. Add noodles, stir fry 45 seconds. Pour in sauce and toss to coat. Add 2 Tbsp water and stir fry until noodles are glossy.
Turn off heat, stir in remaining garlic. Add more soy sauce to taste. Garnish with scallions.

name: Mexican Chicken & Rice

ingredients.name: Lime
ingredients.unit: whole, juiced (add at liquid step, not marinade)

ingredients.name: Cilantro
ingredients.unit: bunch, chopped (mix in) + leaves for garnish

notes: 450 cal / 45g protein / 47g carbs / 9g fat per serving (original macros — black beans and rice adjusted so may vary). Dutch oven recommended. Liquid ratio 2:1 — tomatoes provide ~1.5 cups + 1 cup stock = ~2.5 cups total for 1.2 cups rice. Do NOT add lime to chicken marinade — add at liquid step.
recipe_steps: Season cubed chicken with oregano, paprika, garlic powder, cumin, chili powder, salt + pepper, and olive oil. Do NOT add lime yet.
Cook chicken in Dutch oven over medium-high 6–8 minutes until cooked through. Set aside.
In same Dutch oven, sauté onion and red bell pepper over medium heat (lid off) until softened and lightly caramelized, 3–4 minutes.
Add 1.2 cups dry basmati rice + 1 tsp of each seasoning. Toast with veg for 1–2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add 15oz can black beans, 1 can chopped tomatoes, 1 cup stock, and juice of 1 whole lime. Mix well.
Lid on, simmer on lowest heat for 12–15 minutes. Stir gently at 7 minutes scraping bottom to prevent sticking. Check liquid — add splash of stock if dry.
Check rice is cooked through. If not, add small splash of water, lid on 2–3 more minutes.
Add chicken back in, mix in chopped cilantro stems + leaves. Taste and adjust salt + pepper.
Divide into 4 containers. Garnish with cilantro leaves only.

name: Crispy Chicken Sandwiches
notes: 440 cal / 50g protein / 43g carbs / 6g fat per sandwich. From @stealth_health_life. Parcooked for meal prep — finish on reheat day. Meal 4 only, does not reheat well in microwave.
recipe_steps: Tenderize chicken breasts until flattened. Slice each in half to make 8 pieces. Season with salt and garlic powder.
Set up dipping stations — Station 1: flour + salt, garlic powder, pepper. Station 2: egg whites + hot sauce. Station 3: crushed corn flakes.
Dredge each piece: flour → egg white/hot sauce → cornflakes, pressing firmly to fully coat.
Spray lightly with oil spray.
PARCOOK: Air fry at 400°F for 10 minutes, flipping at 5 minutes. Internal temp ~150°F. Cool and refrigerate.
REHEAT DAY-OF: Air fry at 350°F for 5–6 minutes until internal temp reaches 165°F.
Toast brioche buns at 400°F for 2–3 minutes.
Mix sauce: ¾ cup Greek yogurt + 2 Tbsp + 2 tsp Sriracha + 1 tsp Gochujang (optional) + 2 tsp pickle juice. Adjust pickle juice to taste.
Slice tomatoes, season with salt. Set aside.

TWICE FRIED FRIES: Cut potatoes into fries. Boil in salted water for 10 minutes. Drain and pat dry. Air fry at 380°F for 10 minutes (parcook). Toss with fry seasoning (1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp paprika, ½ tsp onion powder, ½ tsp salt, ¼ tsp black pepper). Air fry at 400°F for 10 minutes until crispy.
Spread sauce on bun, top with chicken, sliced tomato, and pickles. Serve with fries.

name: Rotisserie Chicken + Garlic Rice

ingredients.name: Seasonal veggie side
ingredients.unit: per cycle (kale, roasted tomato + zucchini, bell pepper, etc.)

notes: Meal 3 only (you). Keep rotisserie drippings — key for moisture. Garlic rice base is consistent. Side veggie flexes per cycle based on seasonal availability and inventory. No parsley, no potatoes. CORRECTED 2026-07-10: servings 5->3 (Will's M3 total, 7/wk, is rotisserie(3) + Cheesy Taco Rice #42(4) = 7 exact).
recipe_steps: Cook 1 cup short grain rice per rice cooker instructions.
While rice cooks, heat 1 tsp avocado oil in small pan over medium. Sauté 3 cloves minced garlic 30 seconds. Toss with cooked rice, season with salt + pepper.
Prepare seasonal veggie side per cycle plan (e.g. sautéed kale, roasted tomato + zucchini, roasted bell peppers).
Pull rotisserie chicken into 5 equal portions. Drizzle rotisserie drippings from container over each portion.
Divide into 5 containers: chicken + garlic rice + seasonal veggie.

name: Costco Salmon Milano + Asparagus

ingredients.name: Costco Salmon Milano with Basil Pesto Butter

notes: ON BREAK — rotate back in a future cycle. Take-and-bake — pesto butter comes pre-applied. Do NOT microwave leftovers — reheat in oven or air fryer at 325°F to preserve texture and avoid fish smell.
recipe_steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Place salmon in its Costco tray (remove plastic lid) or on a foil-lined baking sheet skin side down.
Bake 15–20 minutes until salmon flakes easily and internal temp reaches 145°F. Baste with melted pesto butter from tray halfway through.
While salmon bakes, toss asparagus with 1 Tbsp olive oil, salt + pepper on a separate sheet pan. Roast at 400°F for 12–15 minutes.
Divide into 6 portions: salmon fillet + asparagus. Finish each with a squeeze of lemon.

name: Bibimbap
notes: A-tier. Traditional bibimbap inspired. Crispy rice step removed — cauliflower rice provides texture contrast. Store egg and sauce separately for meal prep. Fried egg replaces scrambled for better flavor — runny yolk acts as secondary sauce.
recipe_steps: Cook 1.5 cups white rice in rice cooker.
Brown 2 lbs ground beef over medium-high. Season with salt, pepper, 3 Tbsp soy sauce, 1 Tbsp gochugaru, ½ tsp ground ginger, 12 shakes garlic powder. Cook through.
Microwave cauliflower rice per package instructions. Drain well, toss with 1 tsp salt. Air fry at 400°F for 8 minutes.
Sauté shredded carrots 3 minutes over medium heat. Salt to taste.
Boil spinach 1 minute, drain, toss with salt.
Mix sauce: 5/8 cup gochujang + 9 packets sweetener + ½ cup hot water. Stir until combined.
Fry 1 egg per serving sunny side up or over easy.
Assemble: rice + cauliflower rice + beef + carrots + spinach. Top with fried egg and drizzle sauce.
Divide into 6 containers. Store egg and sauce separately.

name: Spaghetti with Meat Sauce
notes: A-tier. M2 by default, overflow to M4. Reheats well in microwave. Needs large oven-safe pan. 90/10 subbed for 88/12 — slightly leaner. CORRECTED 2026-07-10: servings 6->5 per Will (confirmed real yield). REVISED 2026-07-10: sausage removed, pasta cut 16oz->12oz per Will (targeting ~500 cal meals). New estimate: ~460 cal / 31g protein / 11g fat / 58g carb per serving (fat drops sharply without the sausage, which was the dominant fat source).
recipe_steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Toss carrot sticks with 1 Tbsp oil + salt. Air fry at 375°F for 20 minutes, shaking every 5 minutes until browned and soft.
In a large oven-safe pan over medium-high, sauté grated garlic + diced onion for 1 minute.
Stir in tomato paste, crushed tomatoes, beef bouillon, water, oregano, chili flakes, salt + pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Stir in broken spaghetti until fully submerged.
Transfer pan to oven. Bake uncovered 16–18 minutes, stirring thoroughly at the 5 and 10 minute marks.
Remove from oven, rest 2 minutes.
Divide into 6 containers with air fried carrots on the side.

name: NY Strip Steak & Salad

ingredients.name: Roma tomato, sliced top to bottom

notes: S-tier. M4 only. Porcini powder adds deep umami. Carrot top chimichurri as steak sauce. Light sherry vinegar + EVOO dressing on salad. Use greens medley immediately — most perishable.
recipe_steps: Pat steaks dry. Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and ½ tsp porcini powder per steak.
Place steaks in freezer for 45 minutes.
Make chimichurri: finely chop 1 cup packed carrot tops, combine with 3 cloves minced garlic, 2 Tbsp sherry vinegar, 4 Tbsp EVOO, ½ tsp chili flakes, salt + pepper. Set aside.
Pull steaks, rest 5 minutes at room temp while cast iron heats to level 9.
Preheat oven to 200°F.
Add grape seed oil to cast iron. Sear steaks 1 minute each side.
Place rosemary under steaks, add 1 Tbsp butter/ghee on top. Transfer to oven and finish until probe thermometer hits 135°F.
While steak rests, sauté 4 large bulb green onions in same cast iron with 1 tsp avocado oil. Cook 3–4 minutes until charred.
Dress salad: greens medley + torn kale, 1 Tbsp sherry vinegar, 2 Tbsp EVOO, salt + pepper. Toss lightly.
Add sliced Roma tomato and avocado, toss gently.
Plate: steak + chimichurri + salad + green onions.

name: Salmon Poke Bowl

ingredients.name: Farmed skin-off salmon, diced into cubes

notes: S-tier. M4 on weekdays, any slot on weekends. Batch prep rice (cheater sushi rice method), toppings, and sauce. Portion and freeze salmon individually — defrost per serving throughout the week and assemble fresh. Marinate defrosted salmon 15–20 min before assembling. Nigiri uses 1.5 cups dry rice with traditional fold method (separate recipe).
recipe_steps: Cheater sushi rice: combine 1 cup short grain rice, water per rice cooker instructions minus 3 Tbsp, 3 Tbsp rice vinegar, 1 packet sweetener, ¼ tsp salt. Cook in rice cooker as normal.
Dice salmon into cubes.
Mix sauce: 4 Tbsp soy sauce + 1.5 tsp sesame oil + 1 Tbsp rice vinegar + 2 packets sweetener + 1 tsp grated ginger + sliced spring onion + ½ tsp togarashi.
Toss salmon in sauce. Marinate 15–20 minutes.
Slice cucumber and avocado. Dice pineapple.
Assemble in order: rice → salmon → cucumber → pineapple → avocado → sauce drizzle → 1 tsp sesame seeds → togarashi → cilantro → spring onion.
Serve immediately.

name: Sriracha Teriyaki Salmon + Asparagus
notes: M4. Skinless salmon broiled with sticky reduced sriracha teriyaki glaze. Reserve 1/3 of sauce for finishing. Without skin salmon is more delicate and cooks faster — check at 5 min.
recipe_steps: Start 0.9 cups dry rice in rice cooker.

Make sauce: combine soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, sriracha, grated garlic, grated ginger, and sesame oil in a small saucepan. Simmer over medium 3-4 min. Whisk in cornstarch slurry, simmer 1 min until thickened. Reserve 1/3 of sauce for finishing.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss asparagus with 1 Tbsp olive oil, salt + pepper on a sheet pan. Roast 12-15 min.
Pat salmon dry, season with salt + pepper. Place on parchment-lined or lightly oiled foil broiler-safe pan.
Move oven rack to upper third. Switch to broil high.
Brush salmon generously with sauce. Broil 6-8 min (check at 5), basting once halfway, until caramelized and internal temp hits 120-125°F.
Squeeze lemon on asparagus when out of oven.
Plate: rice → salmon → drizzle reserved sauce → sesame seeds + spring onion → asparagus alongside.

name: NY Strip Steak + Blue Cheese Wedge Salad + Wasabi Mashed Potato

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.unit: Tbsp (1 for steak top, 1 for pan sauce)

ingredients.name: Beef bouillon cube
ingredients.unit: tsp equivalent, dissolved in 1 Tbsp water

notes: S-tier. M4. No porcini or garlic powder on steak (burned previously). Dry brine overnight uncovered. Homemade high protein blue cheese dressing. Wasabi mashed potato with Greek yogurt + low fat milk. Pan sauce uses shallot + butter + bouillon (no pork fat).
recipe_steps: NIGHT BEFORE: Pat steaks dry, season with salt + pepper only. Place on rack uncovered in fridge overnight.
DAY OF (30 min before eating): Pull steaks, rest at room temp 30 min, pat dry once more.
Crisp 2 strips bacon in oven at 400°F for ~15 min. Drain on paper towel, crumble. Set aside.
Make blue cheese dressing: whisk 1/2 cup Greek yogurt + 2 Tbsp blue cheese crumbles + 1 Tbsp lemon juice + 1 Tbsp milk + 1 grated garlic clove + salt + pepper. Refrigerate.
Chop iceberg, halve tomatoes, slice red onion. Set aside.

Boil cubed russet potato in salted water 15 min until fork tender. Drain. Mash with 1 Tbsp butter, 1/4 cup Greek yogurt, 2 Tbsp milk, 1 tsp wasabi, lemon zest from 1/2 lemon, salt + pepper. Keep warm on low heat, lid on.
Heat cast iron to level 9. Add 1 Tbsp grape seed oil.
Sear steaks 1 minute each side. Add rosemary under steaks and 1 Tbsp butter on top.
Transfer to 200°F oven, probe to 128-135°F depending on preference. Pull and rest 5 min.
PAN SAUCE while steaks rest: pour off excess fat, leave drippings + fond. Add 1 Tbsp butter over medium heat. Add minced shallot, sauté 1-2 min until softened and lightly golden.
Deglaze with 1.5 Tbsp sherry vinegar + dissolved bouillon (1/2 tsp beef bouillon in 1 Tbsp warm water). Scrape up fond.
Add pinch chili flakes, salt + pepper, swirl until combined and slightly reduced.
Assemble salad: plate chopped iceberg, top with bacon, 2 Tbsp blue cheese crumbles, tomatoes, red onion. Drizzle dressing. Garnish with chives.
Plate: steak + pan sauce spooned over + wedge salad alongside + wasabi mashed potato.

name: Taco Salad

ingredients.name: Jalapeño, sliced
ingredients.unit: Tbsp (1-2 per bowl, adjust to heat)

notes: B-tier. M4. Meal prep: store beef + refried beans + lettuce + dry toppings separately. Assemble fresh per serving. Only reheat beef and beans — everything else stays cold/fresh.
recipe_steps: Brown 1.25 lbs ground beef in skillet over medium-high heat, breaking up. Drain excess fat if needed.
Add taco seasoning + 1/4 cup water (per packet instructions, or if homemade sprinkle dry seasonings + 1/4 cup water and toss). Simmer 3-5 min until thickened. Set aside.
Warm refried beans in microwave or small pot.
Shred iceberg lettuce. Halve cherry tomatoes. Dice red onion. Slice jalapeño. Slice avocado just before serving (oxidizes fast).
Store components separately for meal prep: beef + refried beans (reheatable) / lettuce + dry toppings (cold).
ASSEMBLY (per serving): Lettuce base → beef → refried beans → tomatoes → red onion → jalapeño → avocado → cheese → salsa → Greek yogurt → tortilla strips → cilantro + lime wedge.

name: Big Batch Baked Ziti (Scaled)

ingredients.name: Beef bouillon cubes
ingredients.unit: cubes dissolved in 1.33 cups warm water

notes: M2 lunch. Scaled to 1/3 of original 15-srv recipe from @stealth_health_life. Original macros (per serving): 610 cal / 45g protein / 72g carbs / 15g fat (using 96% beef — slightly higher fat with 90/10). 90/10 beef sub. Beef bouillon subbed for bone broth. No fresh basil. 6qt Dutch oven recommended. Reheats and freezes well.
recipe_steps: Cook diced onion in olive oil with pinch of salt in Dutch oven until softened and lightly browned.
Push onion to side or remove. Add beef, sear until browned, then break up and combine with onions.
Add tomato paste, salt, garlic powder, Italian seasoning, and black pepper. Cook until darkened (~5 min).
Add red lentils, bouillon water, and marinara sauce. Bring to boil, cover and simmer until lentils are soft and sauce is thickened (45-60 min).
Cook ziti in salted water until just al dente. Drain.
In a baking dish or foil roaster: combine pasta + meat sauce + cheese mixture (cottage cheese + ricotta + Parmigiano). Mix thoroughly. Top with 1/2 cup marinara and 2/3 cup mozzarella.
Bake uncovered at 400°F for 20-30 min until cheese is melted and browned.
Let rest 10 min. Divide into 5 servings. Freeze any not eaten within 3-4 days.

name: Three-Cheese Alfredo Pasta with Chicken

ingredients.name: Milk
ingredients.unit: oz (~0.75 cup) — reduced from 10 oz to cut dilution

notes: A-tier. M2/M3/M4. From Will HOF Cookbook 2025. Reheats well. Slow cooker required. Blend sauce in 2 batches due to volume. Save all chicken cooking juices to add to final pot. CREAMINESS UPGRADE 2026-06-10 (Elena feedback): kept 2% small-curd cottage cheese for cal control, recipe data corrected to 1/3 reduced-fat cream cheese (what Will actually uses, not fat-free), milk 10oz→6oz to cut dilution, blend FULL 90 sec per batch (not just till mixed — graininess = under-blending), supplement with reserved pasta water for adjusting texture at the end. Macros effectively unchanged from Will's current actual prep.
recipe_steps: Place 25 oz chicken breast in slow cooker. Top with 1.5 Tbsp butter, 1.5 Tbsp dried minced onion, 0.75 Tbsp salt, 1.5 tsp garlic powder, and 0.75 tsp black pepper.
Make foil packet: combine 8 garlic cloves + 0.75 tsp oil + pinch of salt. Seal and place in slow cooker with chicken.
Cook on HIGH 2-3 hr OR LOW 3-4 hr until chicken is easily shredded.
Remove chicken, shred thoroughly. Reserve ALL juices from slow cooker.
Cook fettuccine for HALF the box time (very al dente). Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining (more than before — used for texture adjustment).

Sauce — Batch 1: divide all sauce ingredients in half (11 oz cottage cheese + 1.65 oz cream cheese + 1.65 oz Parmigiano + 0.85 oz Asiago + 3 oz milk). Blend FULL 90 sec on high until completely silky-smooth (NOT just mixed — under-blending = graininess complaint).
Sauce — Batch 2: blend remaining half of sauce ingredients FULL 90 sec. Combine both batches.
In large pot: combine partially cooked pasta + shredded chicken + all reserved juices + total blended sauce. Stir.

Set heat to LOW. Simmer gently 15-20 min until pasta finishes cooking and sauce thickens. Add reserved pasta water 2 Tbsp at a time if sauce gets too thick (better thick → thinned than thin → over-reduced).
Taste and adjust salt. Divide into 8 containers.

name: Korean BBQ Chicken Thighs + Cucumber Salad
notes: A-tier (estimate). M3/M4 shared. Source: Instagram reel @C95Yi1vI4NB. Marinate chicken overnight for best flavor. Cucumber salad is a batch — flavor improves through the week. Store cucumbers separately for reheating; warm chicken + rice on low heat in pan. SIDE-DISH NOTE 2026-06-10 (Elena flagged): the cucumber salad IS the green companion for this recipe — do NOT add a second green side. Specifically: (1) do NOT reuse #1 Chicken Teriyaki's gai lan side on the same plate (cross-contamination flavor clash) and (2) do NOT freelance gai lan + oyster sauce as an extra side (Elena flagged the oyster-sauce gai lan as off-putting). If you want a separate green side on a different night, use bok choy with garlic + sesame oil + soy (skip oyster sauce), broccolini, or just extra cucumber salad. Recipe tagged for=will because Elena prefers #28 Bittman Chinese Steamed Chicken (cleaner prep, same cucumber salad on side).
recipe_steps: Combine all marinade ingredients with 21.33 oz chicken thighs. Marinate overnight (ideal) or minimum 30 min.

Make cucumber salad: thinly slice 3 medium cucumbers. Combine with 1 tsp salt + 1 tsp minced garlic + 1 Tbsp gochujang + 1 Tbsp + 1 tsp sweetener + 2 Tbsp rice vinegar + 1 Tbsp sesame seeds + 1 tsp chili flakes + 1 Tbsp + 2 tsp light soy sauce. Mix and refrigerate — keeps a week.
Cook 0.7 cup dry basmati rice in rice cooker.
Air fry chicken at 400°F for 12-13 min (if sheet pan isn't full) or 16-18 min for full pan. Watch for nice char.
Slice or chop chicken as desired. Divide into 3 containers: chicken + rice. Store cucumber salad separately (serve cold).
Reheat: warm chicken + rice on low heat in a pan. Add cold cucumber salad on top.

name: Slow Cooker Shredded Chicken

ingredients.name: Chicken stock or water + 2 bouillon cubes

notes: M2/M3. Zero-effort base recipe — sauce per portion as you eat. Pairs with G Hughes BBQ, low cal ketchup, sriracha, salsa, hot sauce, Greek yogurt sauce, mustard. M2 (Will, starch-free): chicken + frozen veg + sauce. M3: chicken + potato or rice + frozen veg + sauce. Reheats moist and absorbs sauce well.
recipe_steps: Place 3.3 lbs raw chicken breast in slow cooker.
Add 1 cup chicken stock (or water + 2 dissolved bouillon cubes), 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1/2 tsp onion powder.
Cook on LOW 4 hours until chicken easily shreds.
Remove chicken, shred with forks. Discard or reserve cooking liquid.
Portion into 8 containers with frozen veg medley.
Sauce per portion as you eat — keep low cal sauces stocked.

name: Chicken & Vegetable Air-Fryer Bake
notes: Starch-free. M2 (Will, starch-free) + M3. Uses up remaining freezer chicken (4 thighs + 2 breasts) plus loose produce (zucchini, celery, 1 kale bunch). Sauce per portion with low-cal sauces (G Hughes BBQ, hot sauce, sriracha, salsa). Reheats in microwave.
recipe_steps: Cut 4 thighs + 2 breasts into bite-size chunks. Season with garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper, and a light olive oil spray.
Dice 1 large zucchini, slice 2-3 celery stalks, tear 1 de-stemmed kale bunch.
Air fry chicken at 400°F in batches, ~12-15 min, until cooked through (165°F) and lightly charred. Set aside.
Air fry zucchini + celery at 400°F for 8-10 min, shaking halfway. Add kale for the last 2-3 min to wilt and crisp.
Combine chicken + veg. Divide into 6 containers.
Sauce per portion as you eat — keep low-cal sauces stocked.

name: Chicken Thigh Fried Rice
notes: Mostly Elena. M2/M4. Lightened to 1.8 cups dry rice for the cut (~5.4 cups cooked, 6 servings). Day-old chilled rice is essential — cook 1.8 cups the day before and chill uncovered overnight. Reheats well. Uses up green onion, celery, cilantro.
recipe_steps: Day before: cook 1.8 cups rice, spread on a tray, chill uncovered in fridge overnight.
Scramble 16 oz egg whites + 2 whole eggs in wok or large skillet with a little avocado oil. Remove and set aside.
Sear diced thighs in avocado oil over high heat until cooked through (165°F). Remove and set aside.
Stir-fry minced garlic + grated ginger + green onion whites + diced celery 1-2 min.
Add cold rice, break up clumps, stir-fry 3-4 min to crisp.
Add soy sauce + oyster sauce + sesame oil + white pepper (+ sriracha if using). Toss to coat.
Return egg + chicken, add green onion greens. Toss to combine and heat through.
Garnish with cilantro. Divide into 6 containers.

name: Walmart Beef Burgers + Oven Fries
notes: M4 only — toasted bread reheats poorly, do not microwave leftovers. 93/7 lean keeps cal in cut-zone (~600 cal/serving with bun + fries + cheese). Air-fry fries 425F 20-25 min; pan-sear patties ~4 min/side for medium. NEW 2026-06-06 — first cycle.
recipe_steps: Cut potatoes into fries (1/2 in thick). Toss with avocado oil + 0.5 tsp salt + 0.5 tsp garlic powder + 0.5 tsp paprika + black pepper.
Air-fry or oven-bake fries at 425F for 20-25 min, tossing halfway, until crisp.
While fries cook, season patties with 0.5 tsp salt + black pepper. Pan-sear over medium-high heat ~4 min per side for medium.
Add cheese slices to patties in the last minute to melt.
Toast bread slices (pan or toaster).
Assemble: bread + lettuce + patty/cheese + tomato + onion + pickle + mustard + ketchup.
Serve immediately with fries.

name: Costco Breaded Chicken Chunks & Roasted Pork Loin + Veg Bowls (M2 prep)
protein: Chicken (breaded chunks, 8 srv) + Pork loin (6 srv)

ingredients.name: Kirkland lightly breaded chicken breast chunks (frozen, pre-cooked)
Boneless pork loin, cubed (raw)
ingredients.unit: lb raw (~6 srv at 3oz cooked) — leaner sub for marinated thighs (Costco doesn't carry the marinated variant)

ingredients.name: Frozen mixed veg medley (broccoli/cauliflower/carrots/etc)
ingredients.unit: lb (replaces fresh zucchini + bell pepper + asparagus)

notes: Batch M2 prep, 14 servings. Default split: ~8 srv breaded chicken chunks (reheat air-fryer 400F 5-7 min for crisp, or microwave 60s if texture not critical) + ~6 srv roasted pork loin cubes (oven 425F ~18-20 min to 145F internal, season w/ garlic powder + paprika + cumin + S/P + avocado oil). Alternates days for variety. MACRO NOTE: breaded chunks ~190 cal / 18g protein / 7g fat / 8g carbs per 3oz — violates strict M2 starch-free guideline, adds ~64g carbs + ~640 cal across the week. Track in Cronometer honestly; consider air-frying to crisp the breading rather than microwaving. Pork loin ~150 cal / 28g protein / 4g fat per 3oz cooked — leaner + higher-protein than thighs would have been. Pork loin swapped in 2026-06-06 (Costco doesn't carry the marinated chicken thigh variant; pork loin is ~$3-4/lb at Costco vs ~$10/lb for premium thighs). Pork loin also adds protein variety vs all-chicken/beef week. Veg: frozen mixed medley + fresh broccoli florets, roasted on shared sheet pan with veg seasonings.
recipe_steps: Preheat oven to 425F.
Cube pork loin into ~1-inch pieces. Toss in bowl with 1 Tbsp avocado oil + 1 tsp garlic powder + 1 tsp paprika + 0.5 tsp cumin + 0.5 tsp salt + 0.25 tsp black pepper.
Cut broccoli florets into bite-sized pieces (frozen veg medley goes in straight from bag — no cutting).
Sheet pan #1 (veg): toss frozen veg medley + fresh broccoli with 1 Tbsp avocado oil + 0.5 tsp salt + 0.25 tsp pepper + 0.5 tsp garlic powder + 0.5 tsp paprika.
Sheet pan #2 (pork): spread cubed seasoned pork loin in single layer.
Roast both pans at 425F: pork loin ~18-20 min (pull at 145F internal), veg ~25 min until tender-crisp + slightly charred edges.
Rest pork loin 5 min before portioning (keeps juices in).
Breaded chunks: keep frozen. Reheat per-serving from frozen — air-fryer 400F 5-7 min for crisp texture, or microwave 60-90s if texture not critical.
Divide roasted veg evenly into 14 containers.
Add ~3oz breaded chunks to ~8 containers, ~3oz pork loin cubes to ~6 containers (alternate by day for variety).
Refrigerate up to 5 days (containers with pork loin) — keep breaded chunks frozen separately and add per-serving day-of for best texture.
Optional: serve with sriracha mayo, hot sauce, Frank's Buffalo, BBQ, or Walden Farms dressing on the side.

name: Costco Chipotle Chicken Thigh Mexican Bowls

ingredients.name: Kirkland chipotle marinated chicken thigh meat (pre-cooked, pre-diced)
ingredients.unit: lb bag (~6 srv at ~5oz) — deli/refrigerated section

ingredients.name: White rice (dry)
ingredients.unit: cups dry (~5 srv cooked at ~0.7 cup each)

ingredients.name: Frozen pepper & onion stir-fry mix (fajita mix)

notes: M4 dinner, 5 srv. Bowl format: pre-cooked chipotle thigh chunks + white rice (mixed w/ cilantro + lime juice) + black beans + air-fried frozen peppers/onions + salsa + Greek yogurt (sour-cream sub) + cheese + avocado. Macros per serving with white rice: ~570 cal / 38g protein / 16g fat / 55g carbs (rice + beans + salsa are the carb sources). On cut: this is the heaviest M4 of the week — pair with a lighter Will-solo dinner the next day. Workflow: Instant Pot rice + air fryer P&O mix + just-heat pre-cooked thighs = fast assembly. Refactored from raw marinated thighs -> pre-cooked pre-diced thigh meat 2026-06-06 per Will (Costco deli/refrigerated section product). PACKING FIX 2026-06-10 (Elena feedback — fresh items in Tupperware got soggy/wilted/brown): CILANTRO + LIME juice get mixed INTO the rice at prep (cilantro-lime rice holds up fine 3-4 days). PACK in Tupperware: cilantro-lime rice + black beans + peppers/onions + thigh chunks + shredded cheese. KEEP SEPARATE (serve-time only — these brown/wilt by day 2): avocado, Greek yogurt, salsa/pico.
recipe_steps: PREP DAY — cooked components only:
Instant Pot rice: rinse 1.5 cups white rice, add 1.5 cups water, manual HIGH pressure 4 min, natural release 10 min, fluff.
Cilantro-lime rice: while rice is still warm, fold in chopped cilantro (1/2 bunch) + juice of 1 lime + pinch salt. Stir gently. Let cool before packing.
Air fryer P&O: toss frozen pepper & onion mix with avocado oil + cumin + chili powder + salt; air fry 400F 12-15 min, shake halfway, until slightly charred.
Pre-cooked thigh meat: warm in microwave 90 sec or skillet 3-4 min — it's already cooked + diced.
Drain + rinse black beans; warm in microwave or stovetop.
PACK Tupperware: cilantro-lime rice + black beans + peppers/onions + thigh chunks + shredded cheese.
SERVE-TIME ONLY (do NOT pack — they soggy/brown/wilt by day 2):
- Avocado: slice fresh per meal (1/4 avocado per day). Keep whole avocados in fridge, slice morning-of.
- Greek yogurt: dollop from the big tub at serving time.
- Salsa/pico: keep in small condiment cups OR add at serving from the jar. Never in the main container.
SERVING DAY: microwave the Tupperware (rice/beans/thighs/peppers/cheese) 90 sec; add avocado + Greek yogurt + salsa on top after heating.
Hot eats: assemble all the above as a fresh bowl, no pre-pack needed.

name: Canned Salmon + Hummus on Toasted Bread (Quick M3 Lunch)

ingredients.name: Canned wild salmon (drained, in water, no salt added)
Low-cal sandwich bread (toasted)
ingredients.unit: slices (2 per srv) — likely on hand

notes: Quick M3 lunch assembly, 3 servings. Solves Will M3 gap in cycle 2026-06-06 + 2 srv buffer. Per serving: ~280 cal / 23g protein / 11g fat / 24g carbs. Bread is fine for M3 (only M1/M2 are starch-free). Omega-3 boost from canned salmon (~3g/can). Cheap: ~$2/can salmon + ~$4 hummus tub + bread on hand. Assemble on-the-go: toast bread, spread hummus, top with drained salmon + black pepper + lemon. If meal-prepping: package toasted bread + hummus + salmon separately, assemble at work. NEW 2026-06-06 — first cycle.
recipe_steps: Drain canned salmon, flake with a fork in a small bowl.
Add lemon juice + black pepper to taste. Stir.
Toast bread slices (toaster or pan).
Spread 1 Tbsp hummus per slice (2 Tbsp per srv).
Top with ~3oz salmon mix per srv (split across 2 slices).
Garnish with cucumber slices or capers if using.
Eat fresh, or pack components separately and assemble at work (toasted bread reheats poorly).

name: Roasted Tomato Soup + Low-Cal Grilled Cheese

ingredients.name: Nonfat milk (replaces Milk50 in original recipe)
ingredients.unit: mL total (~1.1 cups — 240mL soup + 20mL white sauce)

ingredients.name: Low-cal sandwich bread (grilled cheese)
ingredients.unit: slices (2 per srv — likely on hand)

ingredients.name: Reduced-fat sliced cheese (cheddar or American, ~50 cal/slice)

notes: M4 dinner, 5 srv. Roasted tomato soup (adapted from @cooklikeimbook — nonfat milk swapped in for Milk50) + 1 low-cal grilled cheese per serving. Per serving: ~256 cal / 14g protein / 8g fat / 25g carbs (soup ~116 cal + grilled cheese ~140 cal). Leanest M4 of the week — cut-friendly buffer dinner. Soup keeps 4 days fridge or freezes well; grilled cheese made fresh per serving. Nonfat milk + Greek yogurt cut fat vs heavy cream; roasted whole garlic bulbs add depth without extra cal. Bread + cooking spray on hand from burger cycle; reduced-fat sliced cheese needs grocery add. Added to cycle 2026-06-06 — first cycle. Will's note: M4 buffer lets him overflow flexibility into M2 (eat planned M2 as M1 some days, fall back to soup+gc for dinner).
recipe_steps: Preheat oven to 425F. Halve tomatoes, quarter onions, deseed + halve bell pepper, slice across the top of each garlic bulb to expose cloves.
Toss veg with olive oil + salt; roast on sheet pan 30 min until edges char.
Squeeze roasted garlic cloves out of bulbs into a blender; add roasted tomatoes + onions + bell pepper + tomato paste + thyme + basil + black pepper.
Blend with stock until smooth (~60 sec). Transfer to pot.
Stir in 240mL nonfat milk; simmer 5 min on low (do not boil — nonfat milk curdles).
White-sauce swirl: mix 40g Greek yogurt + 20mL nonfat milk; swirl on top of each bowl when serving.
Grilled cheese (per srv): spray pan with cooking spray, place 1 cheese slice between 2 slices low-cal bread, grill medium-low 2-3 min/side until golden + melted.
Plate: 1 bowl soup (~240mL) + 1 grilled cheese per serving. Reheat soup leftovers in microwave 90s; make grilled cheese fresh each time.

name: Goat Cheese + Crackers Snack Plate

ingredients.name: Crackers (Triscuit / Ritz / water crackers — any preferred)
Fresh thyme or cracked black pepper (optional garnish)

notes: ELENA-PREFERRED snack/light M3 option added 2026-06-10. ~210 cal/srv (5oz goat cheese + 24 crackers / 3 servings + optional drizzle). No cook, no reheat, no meal prep. Standalone snack OR pair with cucumber slices + apple wedges for a fuller M3 lunch. NOT cut-optimized for Will (high fat per protein at ~10g protein/srv); Elena-eat by default. Will alternative for same slot: #26 salmon/hummus toast (protein-dense). Honey drizzle adds 15 cal but lifts flavor. Goat cheese has less lactose than cow cheese — easier digestion for some.
recipe_steps: Slice goat cheese into rounds (or just bring tub + spreader knife).
Fan crackers on a plate.
Top each cracker with a goat cheese slice or spread.
Optional drizzle: honey or fig jam across the top.
Optional garnish: cracked black pepper or fresh thyme leaves.
Pack: keep cheese in original tub, crackers in zip-top bag, assemble at lunch (crackers go stale if pre-assembled).

name: Strawberry Protein Shake

ingredients.name: Strawberry or vanilla whey protein powder
Plain Greek yogurt (optional, Elena richer + protein boost)

notes: PER-SERVING recipe (scale x2 if making for both at once). M3 snack slot — Will prefers lean protein + veg for M1 not a shake (config.yaml Will.M1 = 'lean protein + veg'); Elena may still use as M1 if she wants. CUT-FRIENDLY BASE for Will: skim milk + whey + frozen strawberries + ice = ~250 cal / 30g protein / 2g fat / 30g carbs. ELENA RICHER VARIANT: whole milk + 1/2 banana + 2 Tbsp Greek yogurt = ~400 cal / 35g protein / 6g fat / 50g carbs. Will: skim milk, no banana, no Greek yogurt. Elena: whole milk + banana + Greek yogurt for thickness. Drink immediately — whey can separate after 1 hr.
recipe_steps: Combine in blender: whey protein + frozen strawberries + milk + ice (optional).
For Elena richer version: also add 1/2 banana + 2 Tbsp Greek yogurt.
Blend HIGH 30-45 sec until smooth and thick.
Pour into glass or shaker bottle.
Drink immediately. If pre-making: best within 30 min; whey separates after 1 hr.

name: Homemade Crunchwrap Supreme (Beef)
notes: M4 dinner, 6 srv. Homemade Crunchwrap Supreme. Per serving (est): ~480 cal / 30g protein / 22g fat / 40g carbs (90/10 beef keeps it leaner than fast-food ~530cal). Will cut note: use 90/10 beef, go light on nacho cheese + sour cream, sub extra lettuce for volume; ~480 cal fits M4. Elena A-tier (Taco Bell favorite, homemade upgrade). Assembly is per-wrap so it does NOT batch-cook into leftovers like a one-pot — cook the seasoned beef ahead (reheats/freezes fine), assemble + griddle each wrap fresh. Pre-travel safe: cooked beef freezes; only assemble what's eaten. Eat fresh produce (lettuce/tomato) early-mid week — do not let a head of lettuce linger toward Friday departure. Best cooked Sat-Wed. Added cycle 2026-06-13.
recipe_steps: Brown ground beef over medium-high, breaking up, ~6-8 min. Drain excess fat. Add taco seasoning + 1/3 cup water, simmer 3-4 min until thick. (Make-ahead: cook beef, refrigerate up to 4 days or freeze.)
Per wrap: lay 1 large 12-inch tortilla flat. Spread ~2.5 Tbsp nacho cheese in the center circle.
Layer: ~4oz seasoned beef, 1 tostada shell (the crunch), ~1.5 Tbsp sour cream, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, shredded cheese.
Fold the tortilla edges up over the center in pleats, working around in a circle, to seal into a hexagon.
Place fold-side-down on a sprayed griddle/skillet over medium, ~2-3 min until golden and sealed, then flip ~2 min to crisp the other side.
Serve immediately. Leftover seasoned beef keeps 4 days fridge / freezes; assemble + griddle fresh each serving so the tostada stays crunchy.

name: Roasted Pork Loin + Veg (Will M1)

ingredients.name: Salt, garlic powder, black pepper, paprika (rub)

notes: M1 breakfast for Will (on cut), 7 srv — 7 mornings (one per home morning incl. Fri 6/19 grab-before-drive) quick breakfast before the drive. Per serving (est): ~250 cal / 30g protein / 10g fat / 6g carbs — lean protein + veg, starch-free, cut-aligned per config (Will M1 = lean protein + veg, NOT shakes). Roast pork loin + veg together on a sheet pan; portion into containers for grab-and-microwave-reheat mornings (or eat cold). Pork loin keeps 3-4 days fridge / freezes; broccoli + peppers hold well, asparagus is the most perishable so eat early-week. Pre-travel safe: cook Sat, portion for the week; freeze any not eaten by Thu before the Fri trip. Leaner sub for marinated thighs; Costco carries plain boneless loin. Added cycle 2026-06-13.
recipe_steps: Preheat oven to 425F. Pat pork loin dry, cut into ~3oz portions or 1-inch cubes; toss with rub (salt, garlic powder, pepper, paprika) + half the olive oil.
Cut broccoli, peppers, asparagus; toss with remaining olive oil + salt.
Sheet pan: pork on one side, veg on the other (asparagus added last 10 min so it doesn't overcook). Roast pork ~18-22 min to 145F internal; rest 5 min.
Portion pork + veg into 7 containers. Refrigerate up to 4 days; freeze extras before travel.
Each morning: reheat ~60-90s microwave (or eat cold). Starch-free lean protein+veg M1 for the cut.

name: Chinese Steamed/Poached Chicken + Ginger-Scallion Sauce + Cucumber Salad

ingredients.name: Boneless skinless chicken breast (1 Costco pack from the 8lb/6-pack)

notes: M3/M4, 4 srv. Poached/steamed chicken breast (Hainanese-style, distinct from #28 Bittman which uses bok choy/spinach) + an OVERNIGHT ginger-scallion sauce (grapeseed oil + soy + a bit of sesame oil, sits overnight to bloom) + cucumber salad reused from #19's recipe. Per serving (est): ~390 cal / 48g protein / 14g fat / 18g carbs over rice (sauce adds most of the fat — Will can spoon lightly for the cut). Elena A-tier. 4 srv = right-sized for the pre-travel week (NOT the 10-srv #28 modular). 1 Costco breast pack (~1.33lb from the 8lb 6-pack). MAKE THE SAUCE THE NIGHT BEFORE — grated ginger + sliced scallion steeped in grapeseed oil + soy + sesame oil overnight mellows and deepens it. Cucumber salad also improves overnight and keeps ~5-7 days. Poached chicken keeps 3-4 days, reheats gently (or eat cold sliced over rice). Pre-travel safe: cook early-mid week, freeze any surplus before Fri. Added cycle 2026-06-13. UPDATED 2026-07-10: #28 (the 10-srv modular duplicate of this dish) deleted - confirmed fabricated, this (#35) is the real recipe.
recipe_steps: NIGHT BEFORE: make the ginger-scallion sauce — combine finely sliced scallions + grated ginger in a heatproof bowl. Warm grapeseed oil until shimmering, pour over (it sizzles), then stir in soy sauce + sesame oil. Cover and let sit overnight in the fridge to bloom.
NIGHT BEFORE (optional): salt the sliced cucumbers, rest 15 min, drain; toss with rice vinegar, soy, sesame seeds, chili flakes, minced garlic. Refrigerate overnight — improves as it sits.

Poach chicken: bring a pot of water to a gentle simmer with sliced ginger, smashed scallions, salt. Add chicken breasts, poach gently ~12-15 min until 165F internal (do not hard-boil — keeps it tender). Rest, then slice.
Steam/cook white rice.
Plate: sliced poached chicken over rice, spoon the overnight ginger-scallion sauce on top (light for the cut), cucumber salad on the side.
Store components separately; reheat chicken gently or serve cold. Sauce + cucumber salad keep ~5-7 days. Freeze surplus chicken before travel.

name: Egg + Egg White Bake with Cottage Cheese, Mushroom, Spinach (Will M1)

ingredients.name: Whole eggs
ingredients.unit: eggs (6 base + 3 extra for the 6-portion cut)

ingredients.name: White onion, diced (baked in)
ingredients.unit: g (~half a small onion, per source video)

notes: M2 pick (moved off M1 2026-07-10 - shakes are M1 now for both). Servings corrected 7->4: Will confirmed real portions run ~double the original 170 cal/serving estimate, so the same ingredient batch yields ~4 bigger servings, not 7. ~275 cal / 35g protein / 10g fat / 8g carb per serving (ESTIMATE, not verified against a label). Also realistically only holds ~4 days refrigerated (baked egg + dairy dish) - covers the front of the week, paired with a backup M2 for the remaining days rather than assumed to last all 7. REVISED 2026-07-11: cottage cheese unavailable at the store, dropped entirely - now just eggs + egg whites + mushroom + spinach. Slightly less protein/creaminess, still a solid dish. New estimate: ~130 cal / 18g protein / 5g fat / 3g carb per serving (was ~275/35/10/8). REVERTED 2026-07-11: cottage cheese misunderstanding corrected - Will hadn't bought it YET, not unavailable. Back to original: ~275 cal / 35g protein / 10g fat / 8g carb per serving with cottage cheese included. PORTIONS 4->6 (Will 2026-07-12): same batch cut into 6 smaller portions + 3 extra whole eggs added (9 total, has 10). ~215 cal / 26g protein / 8g fat / 3g carb per portion. Fridge life still ~4 days - freeze 2 of the 6, pull mid-week. GREEN ONION added 2026-07-12 per Will (recipe was reconstructed from a brief description, may still be missing items he normally uses - confirm full list). SOURCE CONFIRMED 2026-07-12: this is Will's adaptation of a budget high-protein cutting-plan video (egg power bowl: eggs + egg whites + cottage cheese + onion + mushroom, 400F ~30min, 6 containers, topped fresh with tomato/green onion/sesame/hot sauce). Will's version adds spinach and runs a smaller batch (16oz cottage cheese / 9 eggs vs video's 24oz / 12) - kept per his purchases. Bake temp corrected 350->400F to match source (crispier edges + enables air-fryer finish).
recipe_steps: Preheat oven to 400F. Grease a 9x13 baking dish.
Saute mushrooms in a dry pan or air fryer until moisture releases and they lightly brown, ~5-7 min. Set aside.
Wilt spinach (saute or microwave-steam), squeeze out excess water, roughly chop.
Whisk whole eggs + egg whites in a large bowl. Fold in cottage cheese, cottage cheese, mushrooms, spinach, diced onion, salt, pepper, garlic powder.
Pour into baking dish. Bake ~30 min at 400F until set and lightly golden, center no longer jiggly.
Cool 10 min, cut into 6 portions. Reheat 60-90s microwave.
AT SERVING (not baked in): reheat (microwave 60-90s, or air-fryer a few min to crisp the top), then top with diced tomato, extra green onion, sesame seeds, and hot sauce or salsa.

name: Protein Shake with Milk + PB2 (Will M1)
notes: NEW 2026-07-10 - Will's actual M1, replaces a cooked-meal M1 entirely (shakes are grab-and-go, not coverage-tracked per config.yaml/coverage.py - M1 slots containing 'shake' are skipped in build_need). ~355 cal / 41g protein / 11g fat / 27g carb per day's shake (ESTIMATE - depends on exact protein powder brand/scoop size and milk fat%, not verified against a label; confirm and adjust). Added specifically to help close the reverse-diet calorie/carb gap versus a plain water-only shake.
recipe_steps: Blend or shake 1 scoop protein powder + 1.5 cups 2% milk + 2 Tbsp PB2 with ice.
Drink fresh each morning - do not batch-prep in advance (milk-based, spoils).

name: Vanilla Protein Shake, RTD (Elena M1)

ingredients.name: Vanilla ready-to-drink protein shake (e.g. Premier Protein or similar)

notes: NEW 2026-07-10 - Elena's actual M1, replaces the protein-bar M1 entirely (shakes/bars are grab-and-go, not coverage-tracked). ~160 cal / 30g protein / 3g fat / 5g carb per bottle (GENERIC ESTIMATE matched to a typical RTD product - confirm the actual brand/flavor Elena uses and update; macros vary meaningfully by brand, e.g. Premier Protein vs Fairlife Core Power vs Orgain).
recipe_steps: No prep - grab one bottle per day from the fridge.

name: Korean Gochujang Chicken + Sweet Potato Meal Prep (Zucchini/Mushroom Sub)

ingredients.name: Carrots, cut into planks then chopped
Low-sodium soy sauce (use HALF this amount if using regular full-sodium soy sauce)
Chicken breast, boneless skinless, cut into bite-size cubes
ingredients.unit: oz raw (~709g, reduced from 900g/32oz per Will 2026-07-12)

notes: NEW 2026-07-10 — from a meal-prep video transcript Will provided, zucchini+mushroom subbed for the original bell pepper per Will's request. Shared meal (Will + Elena), M4, single profile (not modular-forked — same portion for both, unlike #28/31/32 which were dropped for not matching real cooking). EXACT macros from source (not estimated): 558 cal / 48g protein / 8g fat / 80g carb per serving, 5 servings total. Keeps 5 days refrigerated, freezer-friendly, good reheated (2-3 min microwave) or cold. Sweet potatoes: don't peel before cooking (scrub clean with a brush ~30 sec instead) — much easier to peel AFTER baking if desired. Halving them before baking (vs. whole) cuts bake time roughly in half with no taste difference. CHICKEN REDUCED 2026-07-12: 900g->25oz raw per Will. Veg/sweet potato/sauce held constant, still 5 servings (M4 coverage intact) - only protein per serving drops. New estimate ~515 cal / 39g protein / 7g fat / 80g carb per serving (was 558/48/8/80). Lands closer to the ~500-cal target while keeping the high-carb/volume base. SERVINGS 5->4 (Will 2026-07-12): with chicken at 25oz that's ~6oz/serving (original portion), so veg/sweet potato/corn/sauce scaled to 4 servings too. Per-serving macros back to ~558 cal / 48g protein / 8g fat / 80g carb (original full portion), just 4 servings. M4 gap from the lost serving filled by promoting 1 Spaghetti leftover.
recipe_steps: Scrub sweet potatoes clean with a brush (no peeling), ~30 sec each. Cut each in half lengthwise.
Line a baking sheet/rack with parchment paper. Place sweet potato halves on top. Don't bake yet.
Dice onion, carrots, zucchini, mushrooms into similar bite-size pieces. Toss in a baking dish with the 1 Tbsp olive oil, pinch salt, black pepper.
Place both the sweet potatoes and the veggie dish in the oven at 400F/200C. Sweet potatoes need ~30 min until tender; veggies take ~20-25 min.
Make the sauce while the oven runs: combine gochujang + low-sodium soy sauce + water in a bowl. Stir in the cornstarch immediately to avoid clumps. Set aside.
Cut ~25oz chicken breast into strips, then bite-size cubes. Season with a pinch of salt.

Heat an 11-inch pan to medium-high with a thin layer of olive oil. Cook chicken in 2 batches (half at a time): sear one side ~90 sec, flip, cook other side ~90 sec (~3 min total per batch, don't overcook — cubes cook fast). Remove and repeat.
Once veggies are roasted (they'll carry some liquid from baking), add them to the pan and fry 2-3 min to reduce the liquid and add browning.
Add the cooked chicken back to the pan. Pour in the sauce, cook ~1 min stirring until it thickens to a syrupy consistency that coats everything. Taste and adjust salt.
Remove sweet potatoes from the oven once tender.

Divide sweet potatoes evenly across 5 meal-prep containers (weigh the total and divide by 5 for precision — they lose water weight during baking, so weigh AFTER baking). Divide the chicken+veggie mixture evenly. Top each with ~30g (150g/5) canned corn.
Store in fridge up to 5 days, or freeze. Reheat 2-3 min microwave, or eat cold.

name: Protein Pancakes, Premade (Costco) - Elena M2

ingredients.name: Premade protein pancakes (e.g. Kodiak Cakes or similar Costco frozen protein pancakes)

notes: NEW 2026-07-10 — Elena's M2 variety dish, covers the 3 days/week not covered by the marinated chicken chunks bowl. PREMADE frozen product from Costco, not homemade - confirm the actual brand/product (generic estimate used for now: ~200 cal / 15g protein / 5g fat / 25g carb per serving, based on a typical Kodiak-style protein pancake package - verify against the actual label once bought).
recipe_steps: No prep - toast or microwave per package instructions.

name: Marinated Chicken Chunks + Rice + Veg (Elena M2)
notes: NEW 2026-07-10 — Elena's M2 backbone, covers 4 of her 7 M2 days (remaining 3 are the premade protein pancakes, #40). Low-effort: marinate chicken chunks 30 min (soy-garlic-ginger), pan-sear or bake, serve over rice with air-fried/sauteed frozen stir-fry veg. ~380 cal / 35g protein / 8g fat / 40g carb per serving (ESTIMATE, not verified — confirm once made).
recipe_steps: Cube chicken breast. Combine with soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, avocado oil. Marinate 30 min (fridge).
Cook rice.
Pan-sear or bake marinated chicken chunks until cooked through (165F internal).
Air-fry or saute the frozen stir-fry veg mix per package instructions.
Portion chicken + rice + veg evenly into 4 containers.
Refrigerate; reheat 90s-2min microwave.

name: Hot Dog (Will M2)
notes: NEW 2026-07-10 — Will's simplest M2 day, no batch prep. ~350 cal / 22g protein / 14g fat / 32g carb (ESTIMATE, 2 lean hot dogs + buns + condiments - confirm brand/count against this).
recipe_steps: Grill, pan-fry, or microwave hot dogs. Toast buns if desired. Add condiments.

name: Kirkland Frozen Chicken Chunks + Veg/Carb, Flexible (Will M2)

ingredients.name: Kirkland lightly breaded chicken breast chunks (frozen, pre-cooked)
Veg or carb side (flexible day-to-day - rice, potato, frozen veg, whatever's on hand)

notes: NEW 2026-07-10 — Will's other M2 fallback, 2 days/week. Premade Kirkland product, just reheat (air-fryer 400F 5-7 min for crisp, or microwave). Side is deliberately flexible (whatever veg/carb is on hand that day) rather than a fixed pairing - not the inflated 14-serving #24 batch, a real 2-serving version of the same base product. ~350 cal / 30g protein / 12g fat / 30g carb per serving (ESTIMATE, varies with the flex side - confirm once made).
recipe_steps: Air-fry frozen chicken chunks at 400F for 5-7 min until hot and crisp (or microwave if texture isn't critical).
Prepare whatever veg/carb side is on hand or in the cycle's inventory that day.
Portion into 2 servings.

name: Egg Roll in a Bowl Ramen (Will M3)

ingredients.name: White onion, sliced
ingredients.unit: g (est. - amount not stated exactly in source)

ingredients.name: Instant ramen noodles, seasoning packet discarded
Plain non-fat Greek yogurt (bang bang sauce)

notes: NEW 2026-07-10 — replaces Cheesy Bolognese Crispy Potato Bake (had bell peppers, Will dislikes). From the same meal-prep video (96/4 lean ground beef series). No peppers - onion, carrots, coleslaw mix (cabbage-based), ginger, garlic. EXACT per-serving macros from source: 522 cal / 41g protein (source frames it as '1/3 of the pan' = 3 servings; scaled ingredient quantities up 1.33x to yield 4 servings for Will's M3 gap - per-serving macros are unchanged by scaling). White onion amount wasn't stated precisely in the transcript - flagged as an estimate. REVISED 2026-07-11: Greek yogurt unavailable at the store, bang bang sauce base swapped to mayo (arguably the more classic version of this sauce anyway). Raises fat, lowers protein slightly vs. the yogurt version - fine since the dish's protein is already well covered by the beef. New estimate: ~560 cal / 36g protein / 22g fat / 41g carb per serving (was 522/41/-/-). REVERTED 2026-07-11: Greek yogurt misunderstanding corrected - Will hadn't bought it YET, not unavailable. Back to original: ~522 cal / 41g protein per serving with Greek yogurt in the sauce.
recipe_steps: Add sliced white onion to a hot pan, cover to steam/char without much oil, until caramelized.
Add shredded carrots, mix in to soften.
Add ground beef, press flat into the pan for browning. Once browned, break up into pieces.
Add chicken bone broth, minced garlic, and minced ginger. Mix.
Add the coleslaw mix - it will look like a lot, cover to help it wilt down.
Add reduced-sodium soy sauce, rice vinegar, toasted sesame oil, sea salt, black pepper. Mix.
Separately, cook ramen noodles per package instructions (discard seasoning packet), slightly under al dente.
Add cooked, drained noodles to the pan, mix everything together.
Make the bang bang sauce: whisk Greek yogurt, honey, Sriracha, and rice vinegar until smooth.
Divide into 4 servings. Top with bang bang sauce, sliced green onion, and sesame seeds.
Refrigerate; reheat in microwave (or eat cold).

name: Steak & Arugula Salad with Avocado (Shared M4)
notes: NEW 2026-07-11 - shared M4 dinner, replaces 2 of Spaghetti's servings (Spaghetti reduced 5->3 total to make room). Simple: seared/grilled steak over arugula + avocado, olive oil + lemon/vinegar dressing. Steak defrosted 2 days ahead in the fridge (not freezer-staged same-week like the other proteins). Macros not estimated precisely - steak is protein/fat-dense, roughly ~500-600 cal / 40-45g protein per serving depending on cut size, confirm once made.
recipe_steps: Move steaks from freezer to fridge 2 days before cooking.
Day of: pat steaks dry, season with salt and pepper, let rest at room temp ~30 min before cooking.
Sear steaks in a hot pan or grill to desired doneness. Rest 5 min, slice.
Toss arugula with olive oil, lemon juice or vinegar, salt, pepper.
Plate: arugula salad topped with sliced steak and avocado.
